# Appendix A: Formal Foundations {#sec-appendix-formal .appendix}

<!-- STATUS: Stub — to be written after all main chapters are complete.

Purpose: Carry the formal rigour that the main body references but does not
develop. Each section answers a specific question a sceptical reader would ask.

Planned sections:

## A.1 Rate-distortion and the compression hierarchy
- Formal definition of R(D) and the role of the distortion measure.
- Explicit construction: how the three compression levels map to rate-distortion
  problems with different d(·,·).
- Relationship to @achille_soatto_2018 (minimal sufficient statistics).

## A.2 Statistical complexity: definition and properties
- Formal definition of causal states, ε-machines, and Cμ.
- Relationship to excess entropy / predictive information.
- Why Cμ is not constrained by the DPI (it is a property of the representation,
  not mutual information with the source).
- Known difficulties with estimation in continuous high-dimensional spaces.
- Proxy measures and their relationship to Cμ.

## A.3 The autoregressive loop as sequential coding
- The dimensionality mismatch stated formally.
- Joint source-channel coding: why the classical channel coding theorem applies
  only approximately (message constructed during transmission).
- Token interdependence and effective capacity per token.

## A.4 The Saxe/Shwartz-Ziv measurement debate
- Brief, fair summary.
- Why the debate is about measurement, not about whether reorganisation occurs.
- Implications for the framework's own measurement predictions.

Each section should be self-contained and referenceable from the main text.
Write only after main chapters are finalised — the appendices serve claims
that need to be stable before formalising.
-->
